english-everyday-get/117.text: Your English is good — you get by fine here.

english-everyday-get/117.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-get/117.text: I used to get by with one-word answers. Not anymore.

english-everyday-get/118.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-get/118.text: I'll get you onboarded — same as my SF rotation.

english-everyday-get/118.speaker: HR
english-everyday-get/118.text: We need your sign-off as his onboarding buddy.

english-everyday-get/118.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-get/118.text: Get looped in, get unblocked, get shipped — you'll hear these daily.

english-everyday-get/118.speaker: Riku
english-everyday-get/118.text: Can I get a ride to the station with you?

english-everyday-get/118.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-get/118.text: I got onboarded once too. Now I help others get it.

english-everyday-get/118.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-get/118.text: Everyday English isn't perfect — but you get by, get better, and get heard.

silicon-valley-look/119.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-look/119.text: Checkout conversion dropped 8% since the merge. Can you look into it today?

silicon-valley-look/119.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/119.text: I'll look into the funnel metrics first — something looks off in mobile.

silicon-valley-look/119.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-look/119.text: Look up the old checkout docs before you refactor anything.

silicon-valley-look/119.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-look/119.text: Look out for edge cases around promo codes — legal changed the rules.

silicon-valley-look/119.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/119.text: I'll look into logs tonight and look up the integration spec. I'll look out for regressions.

silicon-valley-look/120.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-look/120.text: Start by looking through the error logs from 2 AM onward.

silicon-valley-look/120.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/120.text: It looks like a race condition — I'm looking through the deploy diff now.

silicon-valley-look/120.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-look/120.text: Can you look over my hypothesis before we page platform?

silicon-valley-look/120.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/120.text: Looking through the traces, it looks like cache invalidation failed twice.

silicon-valley-look/120.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-look/120.text: Good catch. Look over the rollback plan — we ship the fix by morning.

silicon-valley-look/120.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-look/120.text: Next: Look Forward to Ship Day

silicon-valley-look/121.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-look/121.text: I'm looking forward to ship day Friday — this feature is huge.

silicon-valley-look/121.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/121.text: Looking back on last sprint, we underestimated QA time.

silicon-valley-look/121.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-look/121.text: Let's look over the release checklist one more time.

silicon-valley-look/121.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-look/121.text: I look forward to demoing the new onboarding flow to leadership.

silicon-valley-look/121.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/121.text: Looking back on my first month here, I've come a long way. Looking forward to Friday.

silicon-valley-look/122.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-look/122.text: Maya starts today — can you look after her for the first week?

silicon-valley-look/122.speaker: Maya
silicon-valley-look/122.text: I look up to engineers who ship calmly under pressure.

silicon-valley-look/122.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/122.text: I'll look after your onboarding. Look out for the staging deploy window.

silicon-valley-look/122.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-look/122.text: When I was junior, I looked up to seniors who explained the why, not just the how.

silicon-valley-look/122.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/122.text: I'll look out for you in standup. Maya, you've got people to look up to here.

silicon-valley-look/122.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-look/122.text: Next: Looking for the War Room

silicon-valley-look/123.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-look/123.text: We're looking for Conference Room B — the all-hands got moved.

silicon-valley-look/123.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/123.text: Let me look around this floor — everything looks like a maze today.

silicon-valley-look/123.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-look/123.text: It looks like they moved the war room next to the kitchen.

silicon-valley-look/123.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/123.text: I looked around twice. Found it — looks like a proper incident room now.

silicon-valley-look/123.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-look/123.text: Good. Look for the runbook binder on the shelf before the next page.

silicon-valley-look/123.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-look/123.text: Next: Look Back on the Quarter

silicon-valley-look/124.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-look/124.text: Looking back on Q2, we shipped three major integrations.

silicon-valley-look/124.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/124.text: Looking back on my rotation, I almost quit in week two.

silicon-valley-look/124.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-look/124.text: I'm looking forward to the NYC offsite — Wall Street immersion.

silicon-valley-look/124.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-look/124.text: We should look into a better on-call rotation next quarter.

silicon-valley-look/124.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-look/124.text: Looking back on it, every crisis made me stronger. Looking forward to what's next.

silicon-valley-look/124.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-look/124.text: Silicon Valley Look Arc — Complete. Next: Wall Street.

wall-street-look/125.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-look/125.text: Take a minute to look around — this is your home for two weeks.

wall-street-look/125.speaker: Nam
wall-street-look/125.text: I'll look into the risk dashboard before market open.

wall-street-look/125.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-look/125.text: Look out for the 9:30 bell — volume spikes in the first five minutes.

wall-street-look/125.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-look/125.text: Look around the floor after lunch. Every screen tells a story.

wall-street-look/125.speaker: Nam
wall-street-look/125.text: I looked around, looked into the playbook, and I'm ready to look out for anomalies.

wall-street-look/126.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-look/126.text: Look through the 10-K before the analyst call — page forty-two matters.

wall-street-look/126.speaker: Nam
wall-street-look/126.text: It looks like revenue beat but guidance looks soft.

wall-street-look/126.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-look/126.text: Look over my summary before I send it to the PM desk.

wall-street-look/126.speaker: Nam
wall-street-look/126.text: Looking through the footnotes, it looks like they hid a one-time charge.

wall-street-look/126.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-look/126.text: Always look over the numbers twice. Looks can deceive on Wall Street.

wall-street-look/126.speaker: Caption
wall-street-look/126.text: Next: Looking Forward to the IPO

wall-street-look/127.speaker: Banker
wall-street-look/127.text: Investors look forward to hearing the growth story tomorrow.

wall-street-look/127.speaker: Nam
wall-street-look/127.text: Looking back on our startup days, an IPO felt impossible.

wall-street-look/127.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-look/127.text: We need to look into the lock-up period rules for employees.

wall-street-look/127.speaker: Banker
wall-street-look/127.text: The street is looking forward to this debut — don't undersell it.

wall-street-look/127.speaker: Nam
wall-street-look/127.text: Looking back on the acquisition, we earned this moment. Looking forward to the bell.

wall-street-look/127.speaker: Caption
wall-street-look/127.text: Next: Looking Up to the Desk Lead

wall-street-look/128.speaker: Nam
wall-street-look/128.text: I look up to how you stay calm when the market swings 3%.

wall-street-look/128.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-look/128.text: Look for dislocations — that's where edge lives.

wall-street-look/128.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-look/128.text: Look out for the Fed speaker at 2 PM — spreads will widen.

wall-street-look/128.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-look/128.text: People look up to consistency, not hero trades.

wall-street-look/128.speaker: Nam
wall-street-look/128.text: I'll look for setups and look out for headline risk. I look up to this desk.

wall-street-look/129.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-look/129.text: Looking back on your first week, you read the room fast.

wall-street-look/129.speaker: Nam
wall-street-look/129.text: Looking back on SF, I never thought I'd parse a 10-K in English.

wall-street-look/129.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-look/129.text: I'm looking forward to having you on the next deal team.

wall-street-look/129.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-look/129.text: Look into the quant rotation if you want to stay in finance.

wall-street-look/129.speaker: Nam
wall-street-look/129.text: Looking back on it all — Bay Area to Wall Street — I look forward to going home wiser.

english-everyday-look/130.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-look/130.text: We're looking for a two-bedroom under four thousand.

english-everyday-look/130.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-look/130.text: Let's look around the neighborhood before we decide.

english-everyday-look/130.speaker: Agent
english-everyday-look/130.text: This unit looks like a good fit — laundry in-building.

english-everyday-look/130.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-look/130.text: It looks like the BART stop is only two blocks away.

english-everyday-look/130.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-look/130.text: We looked around, and this looks like home. Let's apply.

english-everyday-look/131.speaker: Date
english-everyday-look/131.text: I'm looking forward to trying this place — look through the specials?

english-everyday-look/131.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-look/131.text: I'll look up what 'burrata' is real quick.

english-everyday-look/131.speaker: Date
english-everyday-look/131.text: Looking through the menu, the pasta looks amazing.

english-everyday-look/131.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-look/131.text: I looked up reviews — people say look for the truffle tagliatelle.

english-everyday-look/131.speaker: Date
english-everyday-look/131.text: I've been looking forward to this all week. Glad we looked through everything.

english-everyday-look/132.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-look/132.text: Looking back on your first meetup, you barely spoke.

english-everyday-look/132.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-look/132.text: Looking back on three years in the US, every arc taught me new phrasals.

english-everyday-look/132.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-look/132.text: Look after yourself — burnout is real in Big Tech.

english-everyday-look/132.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-look/132.text: I'm looking forward to helping the next newcomer at this meetup.

english-everyday-look/132.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-look/132.text: Look back on how far you've come. Look forward. Look after your English habit.

english-everyday-look/132.speaker: Caption
english-everyday-look/132.text: Everyday Look Arc — Complete. Take arc begins.

silicon-valley-take/133.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-take/133.text: I'll take over on-call tonight — you looked exhausted.

silicon-valley-take/133.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/133.text: Are you sure? I can take on the weekend rotation.

silicon-valley-take/133.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-take/133.text: Take care of the checkout alerts first — they spike on Sundays.

silicon-valley-take/133.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-take/133.text: If anything pages, take over the bridge and ping me.

silicon-valley-take/133.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/133.text: I'll take over calmly. I'll take care of alerts and take on whatever comes.

silicon-valley-take/134.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-take/134.text: Let's take apart this bug layer by layer.

silicon-valley-take/134.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/134.text: I'll take down every hypothesis on the board.

silicon-valley-take/134.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-take/134.text: Take note of the timestamp — it matches the deploy window.

silicon-valley-take/134.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/134.text: Taking apart the request flow, the timeout happens at the gateway.

silicon-valley-take/134.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-take/134.text: Good. Take note of that pattern — we'll take it to postmortem.

silicon-valley-take/135.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-take/135.text: Usage is taking off — we hit ten thousand sign-ups in an hour.

silicon-valley-take/135.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/135.text: Monitoring is taking up both screens — I can't look away.

silicon-valley-take/135.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-take/135.text: If error rate spikes, we take back the rollout immediately.

silicon-valley-take/135.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-take/135.text: It's taking off faster than we modeled. Scale the cache tier.

silicon-valley-take/135.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/135.text: Launch took off. I'll take up the post-launch watch. No take-backs tonight.

silicon-valley-take/135.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-take/135.text: Next: Take Ownership of the Pipeline

silicon-valley-take/136.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-take/136.text: I need someone to take ownership of the CI pipeline migration.

silicon-valley-take/136.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/136.text: I'll take responsibility for the timeline and the rollback plan.

silicon-valley-take/136.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-take/136.text: Don't wait for permission — take initiative on the design doc.

silicon-valley-take/136.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-take/136.text: When you take ownership, the whole team moves faster.

silicon-valley-take/136.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/136.text: I'll take initiative this week. I take ownership seriously now.

silicon-valley-take/136.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-take/136.text: Next: Take the Heat in Review

silicon-valley-take/137.speaker: Architect
silicon-valley-take/137.text: This design won't scale — someone needs to take the heat.

silicon-valley-take/137.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/137.text: I'll take the heat. I wrote the RFC — I'll take feedback openly.

silicon-valley-take/137.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-take/137.text: You took it in stride in there. That impressed leadership.

silicon-valley-take/137.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-take/137.text: Take feedback from the room, then take the best ideas forward.

silicon-valley-take/137.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/137.text: I took the heat, took feedback, and took it in stride. We iterate.

silicon-valley-take/138.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-take/138.text: Before you go, take stock of what you've learned here.

silicon-valley-take/138.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/138.text: Taking a step back, this rotation changed how I speak up.

silicon-valley-take/138.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-take/138.text: I'll take it from here on the pipeline. You've earned the NYC trip.

silicon-valley-take/138.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-take/138.text: Take stock, take a breath, take the Wall Street playbook with you.

silicon-valley-take/138.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-take/138.text: I took stock of every take-phrasal I learned. Aoi, take it from here.

wall-street-take/139.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-take/139.text: We're taking a position before the jobs report — size it small.

wall-street-take/139.speaker: Nam
wall-street-take/139.text: I'd rather take a risk with a tight stop than sit out.

wall-street-take/139.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-take/139.text: If we hit target, take profit at the first resistance level.

wall-street-take/139.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-take/139.text: Never take a position without knowing your exit.

wall-street-take/139.speaker: Nam
wall-street-take/139.text: We took a position, managed risk, and took profit by noon.

wall-street-take/140.speaker: Compliance
wall-street-take/140.text: The desk will take heat if we miss the filing deadline.

wall-street-take/140.speaker: Nam
wall-street-take/140.text: I'll take responsibility for updating the trade log format.

wall-street-take/140.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-take/140.text: Take note of the new retention rules — they start Monday.

wall-street-take/140.speaker: Compliance
wall-street-take/140.text: You took heat well in there. Fix it and move on.

wall-street-take/140.speaker: Nam
wall-street-take/140.text: I took responsibility, took note of every rule, and we'll pass the next audit.

wall-street-take/141.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-take/141.text: Can you take over the desk until Marcus returns?

wall-street-take/141.speaker: Nam
wall-street-take/141.text: I'll take on the morning book and take charge of the hedge.

wall-street-take/141.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-take/141.text: When you take charge, speak clearly — the floor listens.

wall-street-take/141.speaker: Nam
wall-street-take/141.text: Taking over mid-session is scary, but I'll take on the responsibility.

wall-street-take/141.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-take/141.text: You took over, took charge, and the desk stayed green. Well done.

wall-street-take/142.speaker: Client
wall-street-take/142.text: This pricing is unacceptable — we need answers now.

wall-street-take/142.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-take/142.text: Let's take this offline and regroup in ten minutes.

wall-street-take/142.speaker: Nam
wall-street-take/142.text: I'll take down notes on every concern they raised.

wall-street-take/142.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-take/142.text: I'll take it up with legal before we revise the term sheet.

wall-street-take/142.speaker: Nam
wall-street-take/142.text: We took it offline, took down notes, and took it up with the right people.

wall-street-take/143.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-take/143.text: Take stock — you survived your first earnings season.

wall-street-take/143.speaker: Nam
wall-street-take/143.text: Taking a step back, finance English is its own dialect.

wall-street-take/143.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-take/143.text: Next time, take the lead on the morning brief.

wall-street-take/143.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-take/143.text: You took stock, took risks, and took the heat. That's the job.

wall-street-take/143.speaker: Nam
wall-street-take/143.text: I'll take the lead back in SF. I took stock of everything I learned here.

english-everyday-take/144.speaker: Roommate
english-everyday-take/144.text: Can you take out the trash? It's been three days.

english-everyday-take/144.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-take/144.text: I took off work early — I'll take care of chores tonight.

english-everyday-take/144.speaker: Roommate
english-everyday-take/144.text: If you take out recycling, I'll take care of dishes.

english-everyday-take/144.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-take/144.text: Deal. I'll take out trash and take care of the kitchen.

english-everyday-take/144.speaker: Roommate
english-everyday-take/144.text: We took care of the apartment together. That's how roommates work.

english-everyday-take/144.speaker: Caption
english-everyday-take/144.text: Next: Take Your Time at Dinner

english-everyday-take/145.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-take/145.text: Take a seat — take your time reading the menu.

english-everyday-take/145.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-take/145.text: Sorry, I always rush. I'll take it easy tonight.

english-everyday-take/145.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-take/145.text: In the US, people take their time at dinner. It's not rude.

english-everyday-take/145.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-take/145.text: I'm learning to take it easy after years of startup speed.

english-everyday-take/145.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-take/145.text: Take your time. Take a seat. Take it easy — you earned this.

english-everyday-take/146.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-take/146.text: Take stock of your daily English — do you still practice?

english-everyday-take/146.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-take/146.text: I'm taking on new habits: one phrasal a day in real conversation.

english-everyday-take/146.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-take/146.text: Take it from here — keep using them, not just reading them.

english-everyday-take/146.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-take/146.text: Taking stock, I take on challenges differently now.

english-everyday-take/146.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-take/146.text: Take stock, take on habits, take it from here. That's the everyday arc.

english-everyday-take/146.speaker: Caption
english-everyday-take/146.text: Everyday Take Arc — Complete. Put arc begins.

silicon-valley-put/147.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-put/147.text: Can we put off the refactor until after launch?

silicon-valley-put/147.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/147.text: If we put it off again, we'll pay interest in incidents.

silicon-valley-put/147.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-put/147.text: Put aside two days post-launch — I'll help you put together a plan.

silicon-valley-put/147.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-put/147.text: Let's put together a minimal refactor scope, not a rewrite.

silicon-valley-put/147.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/147.text: We won't put off safety. We'll put aside time and put together a real plan.

silicon-valley-put/148.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/148.text: I'd like to put forward an async event pipeline design.

silicon-valley-put/148.speaker: Architect
silicon-valley-put/148.text: Put forth your assumptions on throughput first.

silicon-valley-put/148.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-put/148.text: Did you put in a request for the extra Kafka cluster?

silicon-valley-put/148.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/148.text: I put forward three options. I put forth the trade-offs clearly.

silicon-valley-put/148.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-put/148.text: Strong proposal. You put in the work — now we decide.

silicon-valley-put/148.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-put/148.text: Next: Put the Release on Hold

silicon-valley-put/149.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-put/149.text: We're putting the release on hold until root cause is clear.

silicon-valley-put/149.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-put/149.text: Put back the launch to Tuesday — no hero deploys tonight.

silicon-valley-put/149.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-put/149.text: I'll put you through to platform on the bridge line.

silicon-valley-put/149.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/149.text: Put on hold is the right call. I'll put through the hotfix PR.

silicon-valley-put/149.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-put/149.text: We put the release on hold, put back the date, and put through the fix.

silicon-valley-put/150.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-put/150.text: Demo is Thursday — time to put your head down.

silicon-valley-put/150.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/150.text: I'll put in the hours, but I won't burn out again.

silicon-valley-put/150.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-put/150.text: Put effort into the demo script, not just the code.

silicon-valley-put/150.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-put/150.text: Put your head down today, lift it up proud on demo day.

silicon-valley-put/150.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/150.text: I put effort into every slide. I put in the hours. It paid off.

silicon-valley-put/151.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-put/151.text: We need this decision put in writing before EOD.

silicon-valley-put/151.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/151.text: I'll put down the three options and the rationale.

silicon-valley-put/151.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-put/151.text: Put across the risk clearly — product needs to understand.

silicon-valley-put/151.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-put/151.text: If it's not in writing, it didn't happen. Put it in the RFC.

silicon-valley-put/151.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/151.text: I put it in writing, put down every trade-off, and put across the recommendation.

silicon-valley-put/152.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-put/152.text: Let's put the launch drama to rest in this retro.

silicon-valley-put/152.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/152.text: I finally put the pieces together — comms failed, not code.

silicon-valley-put/152.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-put/152.text: We put it behind us and ship smarter next time.

silicon-valley-put/152.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-put/152.text: Put to rest the blame game. Put the pieces together and move on.

silicon-valley-put/152.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-put/152.text: We put it behind us. I put the pieces together. SF put-phrasals — done.

wall-street-put/153.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-put/153.text: We're putting money on this merger arb — size carefully.

wall-street-put/153.speaker: Nam
wall-street-put/153.text: How much capital are we putting at risk?

wall-street-put/153.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-put/153.text: Put aside ten percent as a buffer for volatility.

wall-street-put/153.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-put/153.text: Never put money on a trade you can't explain in one sentence.

wall-street-put/153.speaker: Nam
wall-street-put/153.text: We put aside reserves, knew what we put at risk, and sized the bet.

wall-street-put/154.speaker: Banker
wall-street-put/154.text: We put forward the growth story by 10 AM tomorrow.

wall-street-put/154.speaker: Nam
wall-street-put/154.text: I put together the tech diligence appendix last night.

wall-street-put/154.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-put/154.text: Put forth the moat argument on slide seven.

wall-street-put/154.speaker: Banker
wall-street-put/154.text: This pitch puts forward a credible path to profitability.

wall-street-put/154.speaker: Nam
wall-street-put/154.text: We put together the deck, put forth the numbers, put forward our best case.

wall-street-put/154.speaker: Caption
wall-street-put/154.text: Next: Put the Deal on Hold

wall-street-put/155.speaker: Banker
wall-street-put/155.text: Regulators put the deal on hold pending review.

wall-street-put/155.speaker: Nam
wall-street-put/155.text: Should we put back the signing date two weeks?

wall-street-put/155.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-put/155.text: I'll put you through to counsel — get it in writing.

wall-street-put/155.speaker: Banker
wall-street-put/155.text: When deals get put on hold, stay patient and stay ready.

wall-street-put/155.speaker: Nam
wall-street-put/155.text: We put back the timeline, put through the docs, and waited it out.

wall-street-put/155.speaker: Caption
wall-street-put/155.text: Next: Put It All on the Table

wall-street-put/156.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-put/156.text: Put all our concerns on the table now — no surprises later.

wall-street-put/156.speaker: Nam
wall-street-put/156.text: I'll put across the tech integration risks clearly.

wall-street-put/156.speaker: Banker
wall-street-put/156.text: Everything on the table must be put in writing tonight.

wall-street-put/156.speaker: Counterparty
wall-street-put/156.text: We appreciate you putting the issues on the table early.

wall-street-put/156.speaker: Nam
wall-street-put/156.text: We put it on the table, put across our terms, and put it in writing.

wall-street-put/157.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-put/157.text: Let's put the lost trade to rest — lesson learned.

wall-street-put/157.speaker: Nam
wall-street-put/157.text: I put together everything I learned about finance English.

wall-street-put/157.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-put/157.text: Put the tough week behind us. You earned the ferry view.

wall-street-put/157.speaker: Nam
wall-street-put/157.text: We put disagreements to rest and put the quarter behind us.

wall-street-put/157.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-put/157.text: Put it behind us. Put together your SF notes. You did good.

english-everyday-put/158.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-put/158.text: Help me put together a surprise for Kenji's birthday.

english-everyday-put/158.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-put/158.text: I'll put up decorations while you pick up the cake.

english-everyday-put/158.speaker: Roommate
english-everyday-put/158.text: Put away the clutter — he can't see the setup!

english-everyday-put/158.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-put/158.text: We put together something small but meaningful.

english-everyday-put/158.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-put/158.text: We put up lights, put away the mess, and put together a great night.

english-everyday-put/158.speaker: Caption
english-everyday-put/158.text: Next: Put Up With Roommate Noise

english-everyday-put/159.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-put/159.text: I can't put up with the drilling at midnight anymore.

english-everyday-put/159.speaker: Roommate
english-everyday-put/159.text: Sorry — I'll put off the shelf install until Saturday.

english-everyday-put/159.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-put/159.text: Put aside the frustration and talk to them calmly.

english-everyday-put/159.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-put/159.text: I put aside my annoyance and knocked on their door politely.

english-everyday-put/159.speaker: Roommate
english-everyday-put/159.text: We put off noisy work and agreed on quiet hours. Worth putting up with neighbors.

english-everyday-put/160.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-put/160.text: Put your mind at ease — your English is ready for real life.

english-everyday-put/160.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-put/160.text: I put to rest the fear of making mistakes at the grocery store.

english-everyday-put/160.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-put/160.text: Put it behind you — that awkward date taught you 'take your time.'

english-everyday-put/160.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-put/160.text: Everyday put-phrasals put my mind at ease. I put old shame behind me.

english-everyday-put/160.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-put/160.text: Put to rest the doubt. Put it behind you. Keep putting effort into practice.

english-everyday-put/160.speaker: Caption
english-everyday-put/160.text: Everyday Put Arc — Complete. Come arc begins.

silicon-valley-come/161.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/161.text: I came across a bug nobody's hit in two years.

silicon-valley-come/161.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-come/161.text: Can you come up with a minimal repro by lunch?

silicon-valley-come/161.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-come/161.text: Sometimes you come across gold in legacy code — or landmines.

silicon-valley-come/161.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/161.text: I'm coming to terms with how much we don't know about this service.

silicon-valley-come/161.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-come/161.text: You came across it, came up with a fix, and came to terms with the scope.

silicon-valley-come/161.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-come/161.text: Next: Come Up Against a Deadline

silicon-valley-come/162.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-come/162.text: We're coming up against a hard deadline — partner launches Monday.

silicon-valley-come/162.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/162.text: It comes down to whether QA signs off by Friday.

silicon-valley-come/162.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-come/162.text: The team always comes through when it matters.

silicon-valley-come/162.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-come/162.text: If we come up against blockers, escalate early.

silicon-valley-come/162.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/162.text: We came up against the deadline and came through. It came down to focus.

silicon-valley-come/162.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-come/162.text: Next: Come Around to the Idea

silicon-valley-come/163.speaker: Architect
silicon-valley-come/163.text: I wasn't sold on the split either — then I came around to it.

silicon-valley-come/163.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/163.text: Come to think of it, the monolith is slowing deploys.

silicon-valley-come/163.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-come/163.text: Come clean with the team about your concerns — don't sandbag.

silicon-valley-come/163.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/163.text: I'll come clean: I was wrong. I'm coming around to the split.

silicon-valley-come/163.speaker: Architect
silicon-valley-come/163.text: When you come clean, people come around faster. Good leadership.

silicon-valley-come/164.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-come/164.text: Welcome back. How are you coming back from the break?

silicon-valley-come/164.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/164.text: I'm coming out of burnout slowly — boundaries help.

silicon-valley-come/164.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-come/164.text: Take light tasks until you come to grips with the new pace.

silicon-valley-come/164.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-come/164.text: Coming back from burnout is brave. We're glad you're here.

silicon-valley-come/164.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/164.text: I came back from the edge. I'm coming to grips with sustainable pace.

silicon-valley-come/165.speaker: VP
silicon-valley-come/165.text: Something came up — can you walk me through the migration?

silicon-valley-come/165.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/165.text: I'll come up with a one-pager by this afternoon.

silicon-valley-come/165.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-come/165.text: When leadership comes up in standup, stay calm and be clear.

silicon-valley-come/165.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/165.text: The latency issue came up again — I'll come through for the demo.

silicon-valley-come/165.speaker: VP
silicon-valley-come/165.text: You came up with a solid answer. The team comes through when it counts.

silicon-valley-come/166.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-come/166.text: You've come a long way since that first war room.

silicon-valley-come/166.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/166.text: I come to realize — every come-phrasal showed up in real work.

silicon-valley-come/166.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-come/166.text: It's come full circle: you mentor others now.

silicon-valley-come/166.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-come/166.text: Come a long way, come to realize, come full circle. That's growth.

silicon-valley-come/166.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-come/166.text: I came a long way from nervous newcomer to SF leadership voice.

wall-street-come/167.speaker: Compliance
wall-street-come/167.text: We came across trades that don't match the mandate.

wall-street-come/167.speaker: Nam
wall-street-come/167.text: I'll come up with a timeline for the full audit trail.

wall-street-come/167.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-come/167.text: If risk comes to light late, we all pay for it.

wall-street-come/167.speaker: Compliance
wall-street-come/167.text: Good catch — this came across your desk at the right time.

wall-street-come/167.speaker: Nam
wall-street-come/167.text: It came to light early. We came up with a fix before market open.

wall-street-come/167.speaker: Caption
wall-street-come/167.text: Next: Come Down to the Numbers

wall-street-come/168.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-come/168.text: It all comes down to margin guidance this quarter.

wall-street-come/168.speaker: Nam
wall-street-come/168.text: We came up against a liquidity cap on the block trade.

wall-street-come/168.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-come/168.text: The desk came through — best week of the year.

wall-street-come/168.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-come/168.text: When it comes down to numbers, story doesn't matter.

wall-street-come/168.speaker: Nam
wall-street-come/168.text: It came down to discipline. We came through for the client.

wall-street-come/168.speaker: Caption
wall-street-come/168.text: Next: Come Around on the Merger

wall-street-come/169.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-come/169.text: I didn't like the merger — but I'm coming around to it.

wall-street-come/169.speaker: Nam
wall-street-come/169.text: Come clean about the synergy assumptions on slide four.

wall-street-come/169.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-come/169.text: Legal needs both sides to come to terms with the break fee.

wall-street-come/169.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-come/169.text: Once I came clean on my model, I came around to the deal.

wall-street-come/169.speaker: Nam
wall-street-come/169.text: We came to terms with the risk. The room came around together.

wall-street-come/169.speaker: Caption
wall-street-come/169.text: Next: Come Back from a Loss

wall-street-come/170.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-come/170.text: We took a hit — now we come back from it tomorrow.

wall-street-come/170.speaker: Nam
wall-street-come/170.text: I'm coming to grips with how fast sentiment flipped.

wall-street-come/170.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-come/170.text: One bad day doesn't define you. Come back from it.

wall-street-come/170.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-come/170.text: We still come out ahead on the month if we stay disciplined.

wall-street-come/170.speaker: Nam
wall-street-come/170.text: We came back from the loss. We came to grips with the lesson.

wall-street-come/171.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-come/171.text: You came a long way from fintech APIs to equity desks.

wall-street-come/171.speaker: Nam
wall-street-come/171.text: I come to realize — come-phrasals are everywhere in finance.

wall-street-come/171.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-come/171.text: It's come full circle: SF builder, NYC trader mindset.

wall-street-come/171.speaker: Nam
wall-street-come/171.text: Looking back, every arc helped me come to realize who I am at work.

wall-street-come/171.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-come/171.text: Come a long way. Come full circle. Go make something in SF.

english-everyday-come/172.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-come/172.text: Come over tonight — I'm trying a new pasta recipe.

english-everyday-come/172.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-come/172.text: Can I come by around seven? I'll come up with a dessert idea.

english-everyday-come/172.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-come/172.text: Last time I came over, we practiced phrasals over wine.

english-everyday-come/172.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-come/172.text: I came over, came up with tiramisu, and came away full and fluent.

english-everyday-come/172.speaker: Caption
english-everyday-come/172.text: Next: Come Down with a Cold

english-everyday-come/173.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-come/173.text: I think I'm coming down with something — throat hurts.

english-everyday-come/173.speaker: Roommate
english-everyday-come/173.text: Rest up. You'll come around in a few days.

english-everyday-come/173.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-come/173.text: When you come down with a cold, hydrate and take it easy.

english-everyday-come/173.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-come/173.text: My energy came back after sleep. I'm coming around finally.

english-everyday-come/173.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-come/173.text: You came down with a cold, but you came back strong.

english-everyday-come/174.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-come/174.text: You've come a long way since episode one.

english-everyday-come/174.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-come/174.text: I come to appreciate small talk — it used to terrify me.

english-everyday-come/174.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-come/174.text: It's come full circle: you host the meetup now.

english-everyday-come/174.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-come/174.text: Everyday come-phrasals came alive when I stopped translating in my head.

english-everyday-come/174.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-come/174.text: Come a long way. Come to appreciate the journey. Topic arcs await.

english-everyday-come/174.speaker: Caption
english-everyday-come/174.text: Everyday Come Arc — Complete. Go arc begins.

silicon-valley-go/175.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-go/175.text: Let's go over the timeline before we assign action items.

silicon-valley-go/175.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/175.text: I'll go through each alert and what we missed.

silicon-valley-go/175.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-go/175.text: I think we go with the simpler fix first, then harden.

silicon-valley-go/175.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-go/175.text: Going through the postmortem, comms lag hurt more than code.

silicon-valley-go/175.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/175.text: We went over everything, went through the facts, and went with blameless culture.

silicon-valley-go/175.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-go/175.text: Next: Go Ahead with the Merge

silicon-valley-go/176.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-go/176.text: All checks green — go ahead with the merge?

silicon-valley-go/176.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-go/176.text: I'll go along with your call if QA signed off.

silicon-valley-go/176.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/176.text: If anything fails, we go back to the release branch immediately.

silicon-valley-go/176.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-go/176.text: Go ahead — ship it. We'll monitor for an hour.

silicon-valley-go/176.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/176.text: We went ahead, the team went along with the plan, no need to go back.

silicon-valley-go/177.speaker: CEO
silicon-valley-go/177.text: This launch will go down in company history.

silicon-valley-go/177.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/177.text: The alert went off during rehearsal — everything that could go wrong did.

silicon-valley-go/177.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-go/177.text: When demos go wrong in rehearsal, they go right on stage.

silicon-valley-go/177.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-go/177.text: Nothing went wrong live. This day will go down as our best.

silicon-valley-go/177.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/177.text: It went off perfectly. Nothing went wrong. This goes down in history.

silicon-valley-go/177.speaker: Caption
silicon-valley-go/177.text: Next: Go Through with the Cut

silicon-valley-go/178.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-go/178.text: We have to go through with the scope cut — no time for polish.

silicon-valley-go/178.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/178.text: Let me go over what's in versus out one last time.

silicon-valley-go/178.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-go/178.text: Sometimes you go with the flow and ship the MVP.

silicon-valley-go/178.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-go/178.text: Going through with cuts hurts, but missing launch hurts more.

silicon-valley-go/178.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/178.text: We went over the list, went through with the cut, and went with the flow.

silicon-valley-go/179.speaker: Architect
silicon-valley-go/179.text: Today we go deep on the data consistency model.

silicon-valley-go/179.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/179.text: I want to go beyond the happy path — what about partition?

silicon-valley-go/179.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-go/179.text: Go over the CAP trade-offs before we pick a store.

silicon-valley-go/179.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-go/179.text: Going deep on this now saves us three incidents later.

silicon-valley-go/179.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/179.text: We went deep on consistency, went beyond basics, went over every edge case.

silicon-valley-go/180.speaker: Kenji
silicon-valley-go/180.text: Clear communication goes a long way in every team.

silicon-valley-go/180.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/180.text: Humility and curiosity go hand in hand — you taught me that.

silicon-valley-go/180.speaker: PM
silicon-valley-go/180.text: You always go the extra mile on docs. We'll miss you.

silicon-valley-go/180.speaker: Aoi
silicon-valley-go/180.text: Go-phrasals go a long way in postmortems and standups alike.

silicon-valley-go/180.speaker: Nam
silicon-valley-go/180.text: I go the extra mile now because this team went the extra mile for me.

wall-street-go/181.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-go/181.text: Go over the EPS beat before the bell.

wall-street-go/181.speaker: Nam
wall-street-go/181.text: I'll go through management commentary on the call.

wall-street-go/181.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-go/181.text: I'm going with long bias if guidance holds.

wall-street-go/181.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-go/181.text: Never go with a trade until you go through the risk limits.

wall-street-go/181.speaker: Nam
wall-street-go/181.text: We went over earnings, went through the model, and went with conviction.

wall-street-go/181.speaker: Caption
wall-street-go/181.text: Next: Go Ahead with the Block Trade

wall-street-go/182.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-go/182.text: We have clearance — go ahead with the block.

wall-street-go/182.speaker: Nam
wall-street-go/182.text: I'll go along with your slice strategy on the order.

wall-street-go/182.speaker: Compliance
wall-street-go/182.text: Don't go back on the limit we agreed this morning.

wall-street-go/182.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-go/182.text: Go ahead at the open — smooth execution only.

wall-street-go/182.speaker: Nam
wall-street-go/182.text: We went ahead, went along with the plan, and nobody went back on their word.

wall-street-go/182.speaker: Caption
wall-street-go/182.text: Next: Go Down with the Market

wall-street-go/183.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-go/183.text: The index went down three percent in twenty minutes.

wall-street-go/183.speaker: Nam
wall-street-go/183.text: My stop went off — at least risk was capped.

wall-street-go/183.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-go/183.text: When macro goes wrong, cut size and breathe.

wall-street-go/183.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-go/183.text: Everything went down together — no place to hide.

wall-street-go/183.speaker: Nam
wall-street-go/183.text: The market went down, stops went off, but nothing went wrong on process.

wall-street-go/184.speaker: Compliance
wall-street-go/184.text: We'll go through every trade from Q3.

wall-street-go/184.speaker: Nam
wall-street-go/184.text: I'll go over the log format fixes we shipped.

wall-street-go/184.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-go/184.text: On audit week, we go by the book — no shortcuts.

wall-street-go/184.speaker: Compliance
wall-street-go/184.text: You went through the review clean. Well done.

wall-street-go/184.speaker: Nam
wall-street-go/184.text: We went through audit, went over every gap, and went by the book.

wall-street-go/185.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-go/185.text: Preparation goes a long way on this floor.

wall-street-go/185.speaker: Nam
wall-street-go/185.text: Discipline and humility go hand in hand in trading.

wall-street-go/185.speaker: Analyst
wall-street-go/185.text: You went the extra mile on every brief — thank you.

wall-street-go/185.speaker: Desk Lead
wall-street-go/185.text: Go-phrasals go a long way in client calls too.

wall-street-go/185.speaker: Nam
wall-street-go/185.text: I'll go the extra mile back in SF. Wall Street go-arc — complete.

english-everyday-go/186.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-go/186.text: Let's go over the list before we split up.

english-everyday-go/186.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-go/186.text: I'll go through aisle five for snacks.

english-everyday-go/186.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-go/186.text: Let's go with the cheaper olive oil — same brand.

english-everyday-go/186.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-go/186.text: Going over the list saved us twenty dollars.

english-everyday-go/186.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-go/186.text: We went over, went through every aisle, and went with smart choices.

english-everyday-go/187.speaker: Friend
english-everyday-go/187.text: Want to go out tonight? There's live music in the Mission.

english-everyday-go/187.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-go/187.text: I'll go along with whatever you pick — I need to decompress.

english-everyday-go/187.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-go/187.text: Go for it — ask the band if they take requests!

english-everyday-go/187.speaker: Friend
english-everyday-go/187.text: Going out in English used to scare me too. You go for it now.

english-everyday-go/187.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-go/187.text: We went out, I went for karaoke, and went along with the vibe.

english-everyday-go/188.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-go/188.text: Consistent practice goes a long way — you proved it.

english-everyday-go/188.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-go/188.text: Manga stories and real life go hand in hand in this project.

english-everyday-go/188.speaker: Linh
english-everyday-go/188.text: Go the extra mile: teach one friend a phrasal this week.

english-everyday-go/188.speaker: Nam
english-everyday-go/188.text: Look, take, put, come, go — they all go hand in hand now.

english-everyday-go/188.speaker: Kenji
english-everyday-go/188.text: Go the extra mile into topic arcs. Customer support awaits.

english-everyday-go/188.speaker: Caption
english-everyday-go/188.text: Everyday Go Arc — Complete. Topic arcs begin.

customer-support/189.speaker: Lead
customer-support/189.text: When a customer is upset, look into the account history first.

customer-support/189.speaker: Nam
customer-support/189.text: I'll follow up with an ETA once I find the root cause.

customer-support/189.speaker: Customer
customer-support/189.text: Nobody got back to me for three days!

customer-support/189.speaker: Nam
customer-support/189.text: I'm sorry — I'll look into this now and get back to you within the hour.

customer-support/189.speaker: Lead
customer-support/189.text: You looked into it, followed up fast, and got back with a fix. Perfect.

customer-support/190.speaker: Nam
customer-support/190.text: This looks like a billing engine bug — I need to escalate.

customer-support/190.speaker: Tier-2
customer-support/190.text: I'll take ownership once you hand off the full timeline.

customer-support/190.speaker: Lead
customer-support/190.text: Always escalate early rather than promise what you can't deliver.

customer-support/190.speaker: Nam
customer-support/190.text: I handed off the notes and escalated with clear repro steps.

customer-support/190.speaker: Tier-2
customer-support/190.text: Clean hand off. I'll take ownership until resolution.

customer-support/191.speaker: Nam
customer-support/191.text: Let me walk you through the reset steps on your account.

customer-support/191.speaker: Customer
customer-support/191.text: Can you put me through to someone who can refund?

customer-support/191.speaker: Nam
customer-support/191.text: I'll put you through to billing after we walk through the fix.

customer-support/191.speaker: Lead
customer-support/191.text: Follow up on the ticket tomorrow — make sure it stuck.

customer-support/191.speaker: Nam
customer-support/191.text: I walked them through it, put them through to billing, and followed up.

customer-support/192.speaker: Customer
customer-support/192.text: This is the third time I'm calling!

customer-support/192.speaker: Nam
customer-support/192.text: I hear you. Stay on the line — I'll look into your account now.

customer-support/192.speaker: Customer
customer-support/192.text: Don't put me on hold again!

customer-support/192.speaker: Nam
customer-support/192.text: I'll come back to you in two minutes with an answer. I promise.

customer-support/192.speaker: Lead
customer-support/192.text: You stayed calm, looked into it, and came back with credit. Textbook.

customer-support/193.speaker: Lead
customer-support/193.text: Support is about follow through — every promise counts.

customer-support/193.speaker: Nam
customer-support/193.text: I'll wrap up my open tickets before I leave the floor.

customer-support/193.speaker: Customer
customer-support/193.text: You took care of everything — thank you.

customer-support/193.speaker: Lead
customer-support/193.text: You follow through, wrap up clean, and take care of people.

customer-support/193.speaker: Nam
customer-support/193.text: Customer support English: look into, escalate, follow through. Done.

health-clinic/194.speaker: Reception
health-clinic/194.text: Would you like to make an appointment with Dr. Chen?

health-clinic/194.speaker: Nam
health-clinic/194.text: Can I come in this Thursday morning?

health-clinic/194.speaker: Reception
health-clinic/194.text: She'll look into your records before the visit.

health-clinic/194.speaker: Nam
health-clinic/194.text: I made an appointment — first time navigating US healthcare in English.

health-clinic/194.speaker: Linh
health-clinic/194.text: You made an appointment, asked to come in, and understood everything. Proud.

health-clinic/195.speaker: Doctor
health-clinic/195.text: What symptoms have you been going through?

health-clinic/195.speaker: Nam
health-clinic/195.text: I think I came down with tension headaches — stress from work.

health-clinic/195.speaker: Doctor
health-clinic/195.text: Let's take care of this before it gets worse.

health-clinic/195.speaker: Nam
health-clinic/195.text: I've been going through this for two weeks. I didn't know the words.

health-clinic/195.speaker: Doctor
health-clinic/195.text: You described it clearly. We'll take care of you.

health-clinic/196.speaker: Admin
health-clinic/196.text: Please fill out both sides of the insurance form.

health-clinic/196.speaker: Nam
health-clinic/196.text: Should I put down my employer or personal plan?

health-clinic/196.speaker: Admin
health-clinic/196.text: Look over section three — signature and date.

health-clinic/196.speaker: Nam
health-clinic/196.text: I filled out every field and looked over it twice.

health-clinic/196.speaker: Linh
health-clinic/196.text: US forms are scary until you fill out, look over, and put down the right info.

health-clinic/197.speaker: Doctor
health-clinic/197.text: Thanks for coming back for the follow-up.

health-clinic/197.speaker: Nam
health-clinic/197.text: Should I come back if symptoms return?

health-clinic/197.speaker: Doctor
health-clinic/197.text: We'll look into the labs again if anything changes.

health-clinic/197.speaker: Nam
health-clinic/197.text: The follow-up visit was shorter — I knew the vocabulary.

health-clinic/197.speaker: Doctor
health-clinic/197.text: You follow up well. That's half of healthcare in America.

health-clinic/198.speaker: Pharmacist
health-clinic/198.text: Take it easy for a few days — hydration helps.

health-clinic/198.speaker: Nam
health-clinic/198.text: I'll look after myself better this sprint.

health-clinic/198.speaker: Doctor
health-clinic/198.text: You'll come around faster if you sleep eight hours.

health-clinic/198.speaker: Nam
health-clinic/198.text: Health English: appointment, symptoms, forms, follow-up. I come around confident.

health-clinic/198.speaker: Linh
health-clinic/198.text: Look after yourself. Take it easy. You'll come around — always do.

side-project/199.speaker: Kenji
side-project/199.text: You're taking on a side project after Big Tech hours?

side-project/199.speaker: Nam
side-project/199.text: I'll put together an MVP in two weekends — go for it or regret it.

side-project/199.speaker: Linh
side-project/199.text: If you take on too much, burnout comes back.

side-project/199.speaker: Nam
side-project/199.text: I'm taking on one small idea. I'll put together a landing page first.

side-project/199.speaker: Kenji
side-project/199.text: Go for it. Put together something ugly that works.

side-project/200.speaker: User
side-project/200.text: The onboarding confused me — I got lost on step two.

side-project/200.speaker: Nam
side-project/200.text: I'll take feedback openly. Let me go through your notes tonight.

side-project/200.speaker: Kenji
side-project/200.text: Can you come up with three fixes before next beta?

side-project/200.speaker: Nam
side-project/200.text: I went through every comment and came up with a simpler flow.

side-project/200.speaker: User
side-project/200.text: You take feedback well. Most founders get defensive.

side-project/201.speaker: Nam
side-project/201.text: We're going live in ten minutes — my hands are shaking.

side-project/201.speaker: Linh
side-project/201.text: Look out for error spikes in the first hour.

side-project/201.speaker: Kenji
side-project/201.text: If the site goes down, you fix it. That's launch day.

side-project/201.speaker: Nam
side-project/201.text: It went live! Nothing went down — yet. I'm looking out for every alert.

side-project/201.speaker: Linh
side-project/201.text: You went live calm. You looked out for users. Launch day survived.

side-project/202.speaker: Kenji
side-project/202.text: Retention is soft — go back to onboarding and simplify.

side-project/202.speaker: Nam
side-project/202.text: I'll put together a patch release this weekend.

side-project/202.speaker: User email
side-project/202.text: Can you come up with dark mode?

side-project/202.speaker: Nam
side-project/202.text: We went back to basics, put together v1.1, came up with two quick wins.

side-project/202.speaker: Kenji
side-project/202.text: Side projects live on iterate, iterate, iterate.

side-project/203.speaker: Nam
side-project/203.text: Taking stock — one hundred users, zero revenue, infinite learning.

side-project/203.speaker: Audience
side-project/203.text: Your launch post went a long way — we saw it on HN.

side-project/203.speaker: Kenji
side-project/203.text: Look forward to v2, but celebrate v1 tonight.

side-project/203.speaker: Nam
side-project/203.text: Side project English: take on, feedback, go live, iterate.

side-project/203.speaker: Linh
side-project/203.text: Take stock, go a long way, look forward. Indie life suits you.

remote-async/204.speaker: NYC
remote-async/204.text: I'll get back to you on the RFC when I'm online at 6 AM your time.

remote-async/204.speaker: Nam
remote-async/204.text: I'll follow up on the deployment thread before standup.

remote-async/204.speaker: Kenji
remote-async/204.text: Async only works if everyone comes through on commitments.

remote-async/204.speaker: Nam
remote-async/204.text: I posted the update — no meeting needed. Follow up if blocked.

remote-async/204.speaker: NYC
remote-async/204.text: You came through on the doc. Async standup win.

remote-async/205.speaker: Kenji
remote-async/205.text: Put the decision in writing — no verbal-only agreements.

remote-async/205.speaker: Nam
remote-async/205.text: I'll go over every comment before we lock the RFC.

remote-async/205.speaker: Aoi
remote-async/205.text: Take note of the latency concern on page three.

remote-async/205.speaker: Nam
remote-async/205.text: We put it in writing, went over objections, took note of every risk.

remote-async/206.speaker: London
remote-async/206.text: Sorry — I forgot to hand off the pager properly.

remote-async/206.speaker: Nam
remote-async/206.text: I'll pick up the incident — what do I need to know?

remote-async/206.speaker: Kenji
remote-async/206.text: When handoffs fail, someone must come through for the team.

remote-async/206.speaker: Nam
remote-async/206.text: I picked up at 5 AM, handled it, and fixed the handoff doc.

remote-async/206.speaker: London
remote-async/206.text: You came through for us. I'll never miss handoff again.

remote-async/207.speaker: Kenji
remote-async/207.text: We go async on updates — meetings are for conflict only.

remote-async/207.speaker: Nam
remote-async/207.text: I'll take ownership of the handoff checklist going forward.

remote-async/207.speaker: Aoi
remote-async/207.text: Look ahead: document everything you'd say in a hallway chat.

remote-async/207.speaker: Nam
remote-async/207.text: Remote English: follow up, put in writing, hand off, pick up.

remote-async/207.speaker: Kenji
remote-async/207.text: Go async, take ownership, look ahead. You're remote-ready.

phrasal-review/208.speaker: Kenji
phrasal-review/208.text: Quick drill — look into or take on a new service?

phrasal-review/208.speaker: Nam
phrasal-review/208.text: Look into the bug. Take on the migration project.

phrasal-review/208.speaker: Kenji
phrasal-review/208.text: Look out for regressions. Take care of the on-call handoff.

phrasal-review/208.speaker: Nam
phrasal-review/208.text: Look forward to ship day. Take stock after launch.

phrasal-review/208.speaker: Kenji
phrasal-review/208.text: Look and take — nailed it. Put and come tomorrow.

phrasal-review/209.speaker: Kenji
phrasal-review/209.text: Don't put off what came up in standup.

phrasal-review/209.speaker: Nam
phrasal-review/209.text: I'll come through on the fix and go through QA tonight.

phrasal-review/209.speaker: Kenji
phrasal-review/209.text: Put forward the RFC, then go ahead after approval.

phrasal-review/209.speaker: Nam
phrasal-review/209.text: Put, come, go — different prepositions, different worlds.

phrasal-review/209.speaker: Kenji
phrasal-review/209.text: One more episode. Grand finale next.

phrasal-review/210.speaker: Nam
phrasal-review/210.text: Look into problems. Take ownership. Put ideas in writing.

phrasal-review/210.speaker: Nam
phrasal-review/210.text: Come through for your team. Go the extra mile every day.

phrasal-review/210.speaker: Kenji
phrasal-review/210.text: Ninety-two episodes. Five verbs. One voice — yours.

phrasal-review/210.speaker: Audience
phrasal-review/210.text: You came full circle from shy newcomer to this stage.

phrasal-review/210.speaker: Nam
phrasal-review/210.text: Episodes 119 to 210 — look, take, put, come, go. It goes a long way. Thank you.

phrasal-review/210.speaker: Caption
phrasal-review/210.text: Phrasal Review Grand Finale — Episodes 119–210 Complete.
